content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Updated the backlog instead of source code. I corrected the release-profile benchmark command and recorded the measured debug-vs-release results in `ralph-burning-juj`, moved its acceptance criteria into the structured field, and created a new blocking P1 task `ralph-burning-lmm` to profile the actual wait source because the release-mode hypothesis was disproven and the under-15-second target remains unmet.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Update `ralph-burning-juj` to fix the release benchmark command, record the measured debug-vs-release outcome, and store acceptance criteria in the structured field.
2. [Completed] Create a new P1 blocker bead to profile the real stub-backend wait source and link it as a blocking dependency of `ralph-burning-juj`.
3. [Completed] Run backlog verification and final tree validation after the bead edits.

## Validation Evidence

- `br show ralph-burning-juj --json` now shows the corrected benchmark command as `cargo test --release --locked --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_start_releases_lock_on_error -- --exact`, includes the recorded benchmark results, and stores acceptance criteria in the `acceptance_criteria` field instead of duplicating them in the description.
- `br create` produced new issue `ralph-burning-lmm` titled `Profile stub-backend CLI wait sources and identify the real bottleneck`, and `br show ralph-burning-lmm --json` confirms the profiling scope and structured acceptance criteria.
- `br dep add ralph-burning-juj ralph-burning-lmm --type blocks` succeeded, and `br show ralph-burning-juj --json` now lists `ralph-burning-lmm` as a blocking dependency.
- `br lint` ran after the backlog edits and reported only pre-existing template warnings for `ralph-burning-fyj` and `ralph-burning-hg4`; it did not report new warnings for `ralph-burning-juj` or `ralph-burning-lmm`.
- `br sync --flush-only` completed after the edits and reported `Nothing to export (no dirty issues)`, indicating the JSONL export was already up to date.
- `nix build` exited successfully on the final tree.
- The benchmark results recorded into the updated bead remain: single lifecycle test 69.65s debug vs 65.63s release test time, full CLI suite 332.03s debug vs 339.60s release, and isolated conformance 322.12s debug vs 344.41s release.

## Outstanding Risks

- The underlying performance issue is still unresolved: `ralph-burning-juj` remains open and is now blocked by `ralph-burning-lmm` until the real wall-clock bottleneck is isolated.
- Project-wide `br lint` is still not clean because of pre-existing template warnings on `ralph-burning-fyj` and `ralph-burning-hg4`; I did not change those unrelated beads in this pass.
- The recorded benchmark data still shows the hot lifecycle test far above target at 69.65s debug and 65.63s release test time, so no optimization claim has been validated yet.
